title: AI Agent Phones Arrive + The Gemini 3.5 Pro Guessing Game
intro: Today's tech is all about agents: nubia calls its NaviX Ultra the world's first AI-agent phone, while the whole industry refreshes Google's page waiting on a still-unconfirmed Gemini 3.5 Pro. Plus a genuinely useful iPhone tip, an honest AI chatbot comparison, and one battery myth worth killing.

posts.hook: Gemini 3.5 Pro is 'launching today' — everyone says so except Google
posts.body: Every leak points to Gemini 3.5 Pro going live today, July 17 — a 2M-token context window (double the field), input pricing that reportedly undercuts GPT-5.6, and a 'Deep Think' reasoning mode. The catch: Google hasn't confirmed a word. There's no model card, no pricing page, and nothing in the public API docs yet. 🤖 The date itself slipped from June after Google reportedly rebuilt the model following internal failures. If it ships today and delivers, it's a real shot at the top of the frontier. Until it's actually on the API, treat all of it as rumor, not fact. ⏳

posts.hook: The world's first AI agent phone is officially here
posts.body: It's official: nubia unveiled the NaviX Ultra and is branding it the world's first AI agent smartphone, launching at WAIC 2026 in Shanghai today. 🚀 The difference versus every 'AI phone' so far is that this isn't a chatbot stapled onto the OS — the agent is baked into the system and can run multi-step tasks across apps on its own, powered by ByteDance's Doubao assistant. And it's real, not a concept: mass production is confirmed and it already picked up a 2026 SAIL award. 'World's first' is a marketing flex, but agentic-by-default is clearly where every phone is heading. The real test is reliability. 🤖

posts.hook: iPhone Calculator hack: swipe to delete the last digit
posts.body: Typed a wrong digit in the iPhone Calculator app? You don't need to hit C and start the whole sum over. 💡 Just swipe left or right across the number display — it deletes the last digit one at a time, like a backspace. It's been hiding in the default Calculator app for years, and it's one of those tiny things that quietly saves you a hundred restarts. ✅

posts.hook: ChatGPT vs Gemini vs Claude — which one for what?
posts.body: Everyone asks 'which AI is best?' — wrong question. Best for what? Here's the honest split. 🤖 ChatGPT is the default all-rounder with the best voice mode and biggest ecosystem. Gemini is unbeatable if you live in Google apps and Search, and its context window is massive. Claude is the pick for writing and coding — clean, careful output. ChatGPT still leads on usage at roughly 54% share, but 'most popular' isn't the same as 'best for your task.' Use the right tool for the job, not the one you're loyal to. 🧠

posts.hook: Closing background apps won't save your battery
posts.body: Time to bust the most stubborn phone myth: 'close your background apps to save battery.' 🔥 Both iOS and Android already suspend apps in the background — a suspended app isn't draining anything. When you force-close everything, the OS has to cold-start each app the next time you open it, which uses more CPU and battery, not less. 🪫 Do this instead: only force-close an app when it's actually frozen or misbehaving. Otherwise, let the system do its job. ✅
